description: Make `wechat-claw tui` expose an explicit SQLite-backed cold-start recovery scene after restart, while keeping operator actions limited to the existing approve and reject flows.

userStories.title: Project cold-start recovery messaging in the TUI screen
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the main TUI screen to clearly tell me when durable work was recovered from a previous run so that I can distinguish actionable pending approvals from informational recovered context.
acceptanceCriteria: Pending approvals render an explicit recovery banner and actionable recovery hint
Waiting-only and failed-only recovery scenes render informational messaging
Footer help exposes approve and reject only when a pending approval is actionable
TUI projection coverage passes in tests/tui/app.test.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Keep recovered thread context visible without a second selection mode
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the event pane to keep showing the most useful recovered thread when no pending approval is selected so that restart recovery stays inspectable without adding thread navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial render uses the selected pending approval thread when one exists
Initial render falls back to the latest waiting_approval thread when approvals are absent
Initial render falls back to the latest failed thread when neither pending nor waiting threads exist
No new interaction mode or thread navigation is introduced
TUI runtime coverage passes in tests/tui/runtime.test.ts

userStories.title: Prove the recovery view survives SQLite-backed restart
userStories.description: As a developer, I want entrypoint-level coverage for a recovered approval scene so that recreating the process against the same SQLite database yields the same recovery-oriented initial TUI state.
acceptanceCriteria: A writer entrypoint can persist a recoverable approval scene into SQLite
A recreated reader entrypoint against the same database rebuilds recovery messaging
The recreated TUI state keeps the correct selected thread context and events
Entrypoint coverage passes in tests/app/entrypoint.test.ts
No boot-time auto-resume logic is introduced

userStories.title: Verify and track the cold-start recovery slice
userStories.description: As a developer, I want focused verification, full verification, and refreshed Ralph tracking so that the branch status matches the implemented cold-start recovery behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Focused verification passes for tests/tui/app.test.ts, tests/tui/runtime.test.ts, and tests/app/entrypoint.test.ts
Full npm test passes
TypeScript typecheck passes
The previous Ralph run is archived before reusing active tracking files
ralph/prd.json and ralph/progress.txt describe the cold-start recovery slice
